inputs.label: Fuel Type
inputs.businessContext: Fuel type determines heating value and combustion characteristics. Based on ASME PTC 4.

inputs.label: Fuel Consumption
inputs.businessContext: Mass or volumetric flow rate of fuel consumed. Directly affects energy input.

inputs.label: Fuel Heating Value (HHV)
inputs.businessContext: Higher Heating Value of fuel. Standard values from fuel analysis or industry tables.

inputs.label: Steam Flow Rate
inputs.businessContext: Mass flow rate of steam generated. Key output parameter.

inputs.label: Steam Enthalpy
inputs.businessContext: Enthalpy of steam at operating pressure and temperature. From steam tables.

inputs.label: Feedwater Enthalpy
inputs.businessContext: Enthalpy of feedwater entering the boiler. Typically lower than steam enthalpy.

inputs.label: Excess Air Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Excess air supplied for combustion. Affects flue gas losses. Typical range 10-50%.

inputs.label: Flue Gas Exit Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature of flue gases leaving the boiler. Higher temperature indicates more heat loss.

inputs.label: Ambient Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Ambient air temperature. Used for reference in efficiency calculations.

inputs.label: Blowdown Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of boiler water discharged to control solids. Affects heat loss.

inputs.label: Radiation and Convection Loss
inputs.businessContext: Heat loss from boiler surface to surroundings. Typically 1-3% for insulated boilers.

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Annual operating hours. Used to compute annual energy and cost.

inputs.label: Fuel Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per unit of fuel (per kg or m³). For economic analysis.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data. Affects output reliability adjustments.

rules: If fuelType is 'electricity', fuelHeatingValue should be 3600 kJ/kWh (conversion).

breakdown.dryFlueGasLoss: Heat loss due to dry flue gases
breakdown.moistureLoss: Heat loss due to moisture in flue gas
breakdown.annualSavingsPotential: Potential annual savings if efficiency improved to indirect method level

hiddenLossDrivers: excessAir > 40 ? 'High excess air increases dry flue gas loss' : ''
flueGasTemperature > 250 ? 'High flue gas temperature indicates fouling or poor heat transfer' : ''
blowdownRate > 5 ? 'High blowdown rate increases water and heat loss' : ''
radiationLoss > 3 ? 'High radiation loss suggests insulation issues' : ''
suggestedActions: efficiency < 80 ? 'Optimize excess air to reduce dry flue gas loss' : ''
flueGasTemperature > 250 ? 'Clean heat transfer surfaces and check for scaling' : ''
blowdownRate > 5 ? 'Review blowdown schedule and consider automatic blowdown control' : ''
annualSavingsPotential > 0 ? 'Implement efficiency improvements to realize savings' : ''

premiumFeatures: PDF export of detailed efficiency report
CSV export of input and output data
Detailed breakdown of losses with charts
